note: Blanks use {{id}} tokens. Validation: 'exact' list, or 'range' computed from the job's REAL material (NOX catalog sfm/machinability) and tool dia — answers change when the material changes. Dialect: Haas/Fanuc at launch (mirrors jobline-gcode extension dialects).

challenges.lesson: Spindle speed comes from material SFM, not vibes.

rpm.hint: RPM = (SFM x 3.82) / dia. Check what NOX shipped you.

zachFail: That RPM would either rub or burn. Look at the material cert on the bar.
SFM times 3.82, divided by diameter. Every time.
zachPass: Spindle's singing. That's the right note for this alloy.

challenges.lesson: Feed rate is chip load math, and chip load keeps tools alive.

cutFeed.hint: F = RPM x flutes x chip load (0.002 here).

plungeFeed.hint: Plunge slower than you cut. Around half or less.

zachFail: Chip load too thin rubs and work-hardens. Too fat snaps the tool. Do the math.
zachPass: Nice sixes coming off. That's a happy endmill.

challenges.lesson: Work offsets and tool length offsets are how the machine knows where the part IS.
zachFail: No offset means the machine thinks the part is at machine zero. It is not. Crash city.
zachPass: G54, G43 H02. The machine knows where everything lives now.

challenges.lesson: Canned cycles: R plane, Z depth, and getting out clean.

cycle.hint: Basic drill cycle, feed in, rapid out.

rPlane.hint: Close enough to save air-cutting, high enough to clear clamps. 0.1 is the old faithful.

cancel.hint: Cancel the cycle before you rapid away.

zachFail: Forget G80 and every move after this drills a hole. Ask me how I know.
zachPass: Three holes, one line each. That's why canned cycles exist.

challenges.lesson: Lathes think in constant surface speed.

sfm.hint: In G96, S is surface feet. Straight off the NOX cert.

zachFail: In G96 that S number is FEET PER MINUTE, not RPM. Big difference near center.
zachPass: Constant surface speed, clamped at 3000. Textbook.

challenges.lesson: End programs like a professional: coolant off, home, rewind.

end.hint: End and rewind — ready for the next part.

zachFail: M02 works but M30 rewinds for the next guy. Be the next guy's favorite.
zachPass: Clean finish. Program's ready to run all day.
